vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1887: feat(flywheel): auto-merge path is GitHub-only — ready GitLab MRs strand under all-tracked-projects scope

narratives.Problem: Under scope=all-tracked-projects with require_uat_before_merge=false the Flywheel should auto-merge ready work in any tracked project, but the schedule path has two GitHub-only gates so a ready GitLab MR (e.g. MIN-831) strands at 'ready for merge'. isAutoMergeEligible (flywheel.ts:325, GitHub-only parsePullRequestUrl + GitHub API state) fires FIRST and 422s a GitLab MR before parsePrNumber (flywheel.ts:333, GitHub-only regex) is even reached. Execution itself is already forge-aware (triggerMerge resolves primaryForge from the merge set and merges via getForgeAdapter(forge).mergeReviewArtifact -> glab mr merge for gitlab).
narratives.Proposal: Make the two schedule-path gates forge-aware. (1) Add a shared parseArtifactRef(url)->{forge,number} parser to forge.ts that matches both /pull/N (github) and /-/merge_requests/N (gitlab) by path pattern. (2) Add a forge column to the pending_auto_merges entry. (3) Swap the schedule handler's parsePrNumber for parseArtifactRef and persist forge+iid. (4) Make isAutoMergeEligible branch on forge, querying GitLab MR state via glab (no GitHub API) for the gitlab path. (5) Lock the already-correct GitLab merge route with a forge.ts adapter test. Execution wiring is unchanged — it already routes by forge.
narratives.NonGoals:
- PAN-933 (review poster cannot post to GitLab MRs) — sibling issue, out of scope
- Unifying GitHub+GitLab state behind a single ForgeAdapter.getArtifactState() abstraction — deferred to the future shared-forge convergence
- Self-hosted GitLab host-credentialed glab routing beyond -R <projectPath> (MYN is gitlab.com)
- Frontend rendering of a GitLab !iid sigil vs GitHub #N — cosmetic
- Modifying the polyrepo / remote GitLab merge paths — already forge-aware
- GitLab/Linear blocker-label enforcement for MIN issues — getIssueLabels returns [] for non-GitHub issues

autoDecisions.summary: Forge detection by URL path pattern, not host (/pull/N => github, /-/merge_requests/N => gitlab).
autoDecisions.rationale: Supports self-hosted GitLab instances; the parser must not hardcode gitlab.com. Host is not needed for detection.

autoDecisions.summary: Add a real `forge` column (NOT NULL DEFAULT 'github') to pending_auto_merges rather than overloading prNumber.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue brief requests it; it makes the row self-describing so a GitLab iid in prNumber is never misread as a GitHub PR number, and back-fills existing rows correctly.

autoDecisions.summary: GitLab eligibility is a focused injectable helper in auto-merge-eligibility.ts, not a unified ForgeAdapter abstraction.
autoDecisions.rationale: The shared forge-abstraction converging with PAN-933 touches the adapter interface and the review-poster surface; deferring keeps this change surgical and the GitLab path unit-testable.

autoDecisions.summary: Use glab `detailed_merge_status === 'mergeable'` as the primary GitLab mergeability signal, with state/draft pre-checks and merge_status fallback.
autoDecisions.rationale: detailed_merge_status is GitLab's recommended single field covering CI/conflict/draft; exact field names are an implementation checkpoint with a documented fallback.

autoDecisions.summary: Do not re-wire execution; triggerMerge already routes merges through getForgeAdapter(forge). Issue work-item 3 becomes a verification + missing forge.ts test.
autoDecisions.rationale: Verified in workspaces.ts (single-repo :5532-5543, polyrepo :5183, remote :4969); the auto-merge executor delegates to triggerMerge, so a scheduled gitlab entry already executes via glab mr merge.

items.title: Add forge-aware parseArtifactRef() to forge.ts

narrative.Action: Add an exported pure function parseArtifactRef(url: string | undefined): { forge: ForgeType; number: number } | null to src/lib/forge.ts, co-located with ForgeType and the adapters. Match GitHub /pull/(\d+) and GitLab /-/merge_requests/(\d+) by path pattern (NOT host) so self-hosted GitLab is supported; return null for unrecognised input. This is the foundation contract consumed by the schedule and eligibility beads — get the {forge,number} shape and the path-vs-host detection decision (D1) right here.

items.title: parseArtifactRef('https://github.com/eltmon/panopticon-cli/pull/42') returns { forge: 'github', number: 42 }, and a trailing-segment URL like '.../pull/42/files' also returns number 42.
parseArtifactRef('https://gitlab.com/eltmon/mind-your-now/-/merge_requests/62') returns { forge: 'gitlab', number: 62 }.
A self-hosted GitLab URL such as 'https://git.example.com/g/r/-/merge_requests/5' returns { forge: 'gitlab', number: 5 } — detection is by path pattern, not host.
A non-artifact URL (e.g. 'https://example.com/foo') returns null, and undefined returns null.
Add forge column to pending_auto_merges (schema v54)

narrative.Action: Add a `forge TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'github'` column to the pending_auto_merges table and the PendingAutoMerge/ScheduleAutoMergeInput types. Bump SCHEMA_VERSION 53->54 (schema.ts:22); add the column to the fresh CREATE TABLE def (schema.ts:508-521) after projectKey; append a `currentVersion < 54` migration block with `try { db.exec(ALTER TABLE pending_auto_merges ADD COLUMN forge TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'github') } catch {}` — do NOT edit the historical v44/v45 CREATE blocks. In pending-auto-merges-db.ts add forge to PendingAutoMerge, ScheduleAutoMergeInput, PendingAutoMergeRow, toPendingAutoMerge (coalesce row.forge ?? 'github'), and the INSERT (input.forge ?? 'github'); import ForgeType from ../forge.js. Update pending-auto-merges-schema.test.ts to assert version 54 and add 'forge' to the expected column list after 'projectKey'.

items.title: SCHEMA_VERSION equals 54 and a freshly initialized DB's pending_auto_merges table includes a 'forge' column (PRAGMA table_info shows it after 'projectKey').
An existing v53 database, when migrated, gains the 'forge' column and pre-existing rows read back forge = 'github'.
scheduleAutoMergeWithResult({ ..., forge: 'gitlab' }) persists and getPendingAutoMerge returns forge: 'gitlab'; scheduling without a forge field returns forge: 'github'.
pending-auto-merges-schema.test.ts asserts SCHEMA_VERSION 54 and its expected column list contains 'forge' immediately after 'projectKey'.
Make /api/flywheel/auto-merge/schedule accept GitLab MR URLs

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts, replace the GitHub-only parsePrNumber usage in postAutoMergeSchedulePayload with parseArtifactRef (imported from ../../../lib/forge.js). When parseArtifactRef(reviewStatus.prUrl) is null, keep the existing 422 'review status PR URL is missing or invalid'. Otherwise pass prNumber: artifactRef.number and forge: artifactRef.forge into the schedule payload (scheduleAutoMergeWithResult). Delete the now-unused parsePrNumber function (flywheel.ts:272-275). Leave every GitHub code path byte-for-byte unchanged. Extend src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/flywheel.test.ts.

items.title: postAutoMergeSchedulePayload with a GitLab MR prUrl ('.../-/merge_requests/62') and an eligible-stubbed isEligible dep returns status 200 (not 422), and the scheduled entry carries forge: 'gitlab' and prNumber 62.
The existing GitHub schedule path returns status 200 with forge: 'github' and the parsed PR number, with the GitHub branch of the handler unchanged.
A reviewStatus.prUrl matching neither /pull/ nor /-/merge_requests/ returns status 422.
parsePrNumber is deleted: `git grep parsePrNumber src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts` returns no matches and typecheck/lint pass.
Make isAutoMergeEligible evaluate GitLab MRs via glab

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/auto-merge-eligibility.ts, branch isAutoMergeEligible on forge via parseArtifactRef(reviewStatus.prUrl). Keep the GitHub path exactly as-is. Add a GitLab path: add an injectable dep getGitLabMrState?(projectPath, iid) to AutoMergeEligibilityDeps with a default impl that runs `execFileAsync('glab', ['mr','view', String(iid), '-R', projectPath, '-F', 'json'])` and parses the JSON (CONFIRM field names against a real MYN MR per the implementation checkpoint; primary signal detailed_merge_status === 'mergeable', pre-checks state === 'opened' and draft !== true and has_conflicts !== true, fallback merge_status === 'can_be_merged'). Parse <projectPath> and iid from https://<host>/<projectPath>/-/merge_requests/<iid>. Reject (ineligible with reason) on non-opened/merged/closed/draft/conflict/not-mergeable; wrap the glab call so any error yields { eligible: false, reason }, never a throw. NEVER use execSync (server-reachable, PAN-70). Leave the GitHub-issue blocker-label check (getIssueLabels) — it already returns [] for non-GitHub issues. Extend src/lib/cloister/__tests__/auto-merge-eligibility.test.ts.

items.title: isAutoMergeEligible for a GitLab MR with stubbed getGitLabMrState { state: 'opened', draft: false, detailed_merge_status: 'mergeable' } returns { eligible: true }.

It returns { eligible: false } with a reason for a GitLab MR whose state is 'merged' or 'closed', whose draft is true, or whose detailed_merge_status is non-'mergeable' (e.g. 'ci_still_running' or 'conflict').
When the stubbed getGitLabMrState throws (glab failure), isAutoMergeEligible returns { eligible: false } and does not throw.
For a GitHub PR URL, isAutoMergeEligible returns its verdict via getPullRequestState and makes no getGitLabMrState call.
Lock the GitLab merge route with a forge.ts adapter test

narrative.Action: Add a test to tests/unit/lib/forge.test.ts asserting the GitLab merge route, which is already forge-aware in production (triggerMerge -> getForgeAdapter(forge).mergeReviewArtifact) but currently untested at the gitlab seam (forge.test.ts tests gitlab createReviewArtifact and github mergeReviewArtifact only). With exec mocked, getForgeAdapter('gitlab').mergeReviewArtifact({ forge: 'gitlab', id: '62', method: 'squash' }) must issue a `glab mr merge 62 --squash` command and issue no `gh` command.

items.title: With exec mocked, getForgeAdapter('gitlab').mergeReviewArtifact({ forge: 'gitlab', id: '62', method: 'squash' }) issues exactly one command whose text contains 'glab mr merge 62' and '--squash'.
The mocked exec receives no command starting with 'gh ' during the GitLab merge, and the existing GitHub merge tests in forge.test.ts remain unchanged.
